name: CloudCannon Git-Based CMS API
what_it_does: Git-based CMS with visual editing for static site generators — enabling development agencies to deliver non-technical clients a clean visual editor for Jekyll, Hugo, Eleventy, Astro, Next.js, and Nuxt sites, with content stored in Git, client-facing hosting, branching for staging, and a developer-focused CMS configuration system for customizing the editorial experience.
use_cases: Providing non-technical clients visual editing for Jekyll, Hugo, or Eleventy static sites via CloudCannon
Managing site builds, deployments, and environments via CloudCannon API for CI/CD integration
Triggering site rebuilds and managing CloudCannon hosting environments via API
Integrating CloudCannon content changes with external systems via webhook for automated workflows
Managing team members and site access via CloudCannon API for agency client management
not_for: Enterprise DXP with marketing automation (CloudCannon is static site CMS, not full DXP)
Database-driven content (CloudCannon is Git-backend, not database-backed CMS)
Real-time dynamic content (static sites require rebuild for content changes)
best_when: A web development agency builds static sites (Jekyll, Hugo, Eleventy, Astro) for clients — providing clients a clean visual editor while keeping content in Git and leveraging static hosting for performance and security.
avoid_when: You need database-driven CMS, real-time content updates, marketing automation, or personalization — static site limitations apply.

auth.notes: API key from CloudCannon account. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. Webhook for site build, deployment, and content change events. REST API for site and organization management. Hosting on CloudCannon CDN. Build pipeline supports Jekyll, Hugo, Eleventy, Astro, Next.js, Nuxt, and custom SSGs. Git sync via GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Basic $45/month (2 editors); Standard $95/month (unlimited editors); Team $225/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: New Zealand company (Christchurch). Founded 2013 by George Phillips and Mike Neumegen. Bootstrapped and profitable. Pioneer in Git-based CMS for agencies. Known for excellence in Jekyll CMS editing. Expanded to Hugo, Eleventy, Astro, and JavaScript frameworks. Strong agency adoption worldwide. Ruby on Rails backend. Hosts and builds sites (not just CMS — full JAMstack hosting). Competing with Netlify (hosting) + TinaCMS (CMS) combined. Profitable niche business.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for site and organization management. API key bearer auth. Good developer documentation. Webhook for build and deployment events. Static site focus means API is for management, not content delivery (content is in Git/static files). Rate limits not documented. New Zealand company with GDPR compliance. US data residency only.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Site reads fully idempotent. Build status reads idempotent by build ID. Deployment reads idempotent. Site creation not idempotent — check by name.
known_agent_gotchas: Git-backed CMS — content is in Git, not served via content API (read files from Git repo)
Static site builds triggered on content change — content available only after build completes
API is for site management (builds, deployments), not content delivery
US data residency only — EU agencies need to assess GDPR adequacy
Rate limits not documented — implement conservative throttling

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. API key auth. New Zealand company with GDPR awareness. Git-backed content provides natural audit trail. Site content is public (static sites) — access controls are at CMS editing layer only. API key provides full account access — protect carefully. Client site credentials must be managed per-client for isolation.
